items.question_text: When should the aircraft be informed it is approaching the glidepath for a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-1 requires informing the aircraft approximately 10 to 30 seconds before final descent.

choices.text: Approximately 10 to 30 seconds before final descent
At the final approach fix
When the aircraft reaches the glidepath
Only when the pilot requests it

items.question_text: If the elevation portion of PAR fails DURING a precision approach, what must the controller do?
items.explanation: 5-12-10a requires discontinuing PAR instructions and telling the aircraft to take over visually or execute missed approach. If a surveillance approach is established for the same runway, inform the aircraft it can be given.

choices.text: Discontinue PAR, tell aircraft to take over visually or execute missed approach; offer surveillance approach if available
Continue the approach using the remaining azimuth guidance
Immediately issue a missed approach with no alternative
Transfer control to the tower

items.question_text: When using PAR azimuth without glideslope, how is mileage information provided?
items.explanation: 5-12-10a2 states mileage information will be from touchdown when PAR azimuth is used for a surveillance approach.

choices.text: Mileages will be from touchdown
Mileages will be from the runway threshold
Mileages will be from the approach gate
Mileages will be from the MAP

items.question_text: May PAR approaches be conducted when the ASR is unusable?
items.explanation: 5-12-11 permits PAR approaches when ASR is unusable provided a nonradar approach will position the aircraft over a navaid or DME fix within PAR coverage, or adjacent radar can provide direct handoff.

choices.text: Yes, if nonradar approach positions aircraft over navaid/DME within PAR coverage or adjacent radar provides handoff
No, PAR cannot be conducted without ASR
Only in VFR conditions
Only for military aircraft

items.question_text: When must Decision Height (DH) be provided during a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-2 requires providing the DH to any pilot who requests it.

choices.text: To any pilot who requests it
Automatically to all aircraft on PAR approach
Only when weather is below minimums
At the final approach fix

items.question_text: When should the PAR controller instruct the aircraft to begin descent?
items.explanation: 5-12-3 requires instructing the aircraft to begin descent when it reaches the point where final descent is to start.

choices.text: When the aircraft reaches the point where final descent is to start
When the pilot requests it
At the approach gate
When the aircraft is established on glideslope

items.question_text: How often should transmissions occur on a PAR precision final approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-4a requires transmissions approximately every 5 seconds on a precision final approach.

choices.text: Approximately every 5 seconds
Approximately every 15 seconds
Approximately every 30 seconds
Approximately every minute

items.question_text: What glidepath and course information is provided on a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-4a requires course guidance, on glidepath notification, on course notification, and deviation info (SLIGHTLY/WELL ABOVE/BELOW GLIDEPATH, LEFT/RIGHT OF COURSE).

choices.text: Heading, on glidepath, on course, and deviation info (above/below glidepath, left/right of course)
Only heading and on course information
Only glidepath deviation information
Only altitude and distance information

items.question_text: How often must distance from touchdown be provided on a PAR final approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-5 requires informing the aircraft of its distance from touchdown at least once each mile on final.

choices.text: At least once each mile on final
At least once every 30 seconds
Only when asked by the pilot
At the start and end of final approach only

items.question_text: What notification is given when the aircraft reaches published decision height on a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-6 requires informing the aircraft when it reaches the published decision height with the phraseology 'AT DECISION HEIGHT.'

choices.text: AT DECISION HEIGHT
MINIMUMS
APPROACHING DECISION HEIGHT
DECISION HEIGHT NOW

items.question_text: For how long must glidepath and course information be provided on a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-7a requires continuing glidepath and course information until the aircraft passes over the threshold. Below DH, this information is advisory only.

choices.text: Until the aircraft passes over the landing threshold
Until the aircraft reaches decision height
Until the pilot reports runway in sight
Until the approach lights are in sight

items.question_text: What position advisories are given on a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-7 requires informing the aircraft: (a) over approach lights, (b) over landing threshold with position relative to final approach course.

choices.text: OVER APPROACH LIGHTS, then OVER LANDING THRESHOLD with position relative to course
Only runway distance information
Only glidepath deviation
No position advisories below decision height

items.question_text: When must precision approach guidance be discontinued?
items.explanation: 5-12-8a lists: (1) pilot requests, (2) safe approach to landing threshold questionable, (3) aircraft passes over landing threshold, (4) pilot reports runway/approach lights in sight and requests/advises will proceed visually.

choices.text: Pilot requests, safe approach questionable, over threshold, or pilot reports lights and will proceed visually
Only when the pilot requests
Only at decision height
Only when the tower accepts the handoff

items.question_text: After a pilot reports runway/approach lights in sight and has been instructed to proceed visually, what must happen to PAR procedures?
items.explanation: 5-12-8c requires that ALL PAR approach procedures must be discontinued after the pilot has reported lights, requested/advised visual, and been instructed to proceed visually.

choices.text: All PAR approach procedures must be discontinued
PAR procedures continue until touchdown
Only glidepath information is discontinued
Only azimuth information is discontinued

items.question_text: When should communications transfer instructions be issued during a PAR approach?
items.explanation: 5-12-9 note states transfer should be delayed slightly until the aircraft is on the landing roll-out to avoid diverting pilot attention during transition and touchdown.

choices.text: Slightly delayed until the aircraft is on landing roll-out
Immediately after the aircraft passes decision height
At the final approach fix
Before the aircraft begins final descent

items.situation: The elevation portion of the PAR equipment fails during a precision approach to Runway 36. A surveillance approach (ASR) is established for the same runway.

choices.text: Discontinue PAR. Tell aircraft: NO GLIDEPATH INFORMATION AVAILABLE. IF RUNWAY/APPROACH/RUNWAY LIGHTS NOT IN SIGHT, EXECUTE MISSED APPROACH. Then offer surveillance approach.
Continue the approach using azimuth-only PAR guidance
Hand off to the next sector immediately
Issue an immediate missed approach with no alternative

items.explanation: 5-12-10A requires discontinuing PAR, telling aircraft to take over visually or execute missed approach, and if a surveillance approach is available, offering it.

items.situation: During a PAR approach, the pilot reports 'runway in sight' but does NOT request to proceed visually.

choices.text: Continue PAR guidance. A report of 'runway in sight' alone is not a request to proceed visually.
Immediately discontinue PAR guidance and instruct to proceed visually
Issue a go-around
Ask the pilot if they want to discontinue PAR guidance

items.explanation: 5-12-8A note states a pilot's report of 'runway in sight' or 'visual' is not a request to proceed visually. Continue guidance unless discontinued per 5-12-8a criteria.
